extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco won solo after attacking inside final 150m on uphill finish to Arinsal, crashed immediately post-line into barriers.
tactical_patterns: CARUSO Damiano at only 23 seconds back was allowed into breakaway forcing peloton tempo, revealing GC team coordination gaps.
KUSS Sepp attacked for VINGEGAARD Jonas stage hunting when already in podium position, showing Jumbo-Visma dual leadership tension early.
EVENEPOEL Remco launched winning move with just 150m remaining on uphill finish, exploiting late hesitation from climbers in sprint.
Bonification seconds at summit of Category 1 climb created tactical split focus between stage control and time gaps.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Won stage and took race lead but crashed hard post-finish; sustainable GC defense with Soudal depth unclear.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Shadowed EVENEPOEL's winning move closely, only rider able to follow late acceleration on uphill finish.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: neutral: Had attacks on final climb but couldn't distance rivals; positioning suggests conservative early GC approach.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Required KUSS pacing to rejoin lead group; no independent attacking shown despite team support.
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Made impressive solo attack while teammates present, indicating freedom or form to hunt stages early.
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: Gained five minutes in break despite being 23 seconds back GC; veteran opportunism forced peloton work.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Moved into virtual race lead position before final sprint, showing strong form early.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Juan Pedro: neutral: Hanging in select group on final climb but no proactive moves.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: Present in finale group but no mentioned attacking or defending actions.
rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: Frontal work shredded group quickly when brought to front on final climb.
